summary: A high‑energy Capitol Theatre show with a scorching "Good Lovin'", but the audience tape sounds fast and uneven, leaving listeners wishing for a soundboard.
review: Reviewers rave about the performance – especially the blistering rendition of “Good Lovin’” that drives the set forward – while noting the recording is an audience source that feels rushed and a bit muddy. The show is recommended for Deadheads who can overlook the tape’s technical flaws, though many hope a proper soundboard will surface someday.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong positive comments on the musical performance (70% weight) offset by consistent complaints about the audience‑tape quality (30% weight), resulting in an overall solid but not perfect rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Tape sounds fast and a bit muddy; listeners perceive speed changes especially on “Good Lovin’”. No soundboard, so ambience is present but clarity suffers.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is praised for its relentless drive and fast‑paced jams, with “Good Lovin’” highlighted as a high‑octane climax that keeps the energy up throughout.
